Meru Health Advanced Convergence in Mental Health

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Daniel Kraft, MD
1mo
  • Report this post

The future of mental health won't be a single drug or app, it will be the convergence of biomarkers, AI, and a real clinical team. Excited to see Meru Health Advanced bring that convergence to a category that has needed it for a long time.

Kristian Ranta
1mo

I lost my brother Peter to suicide. We didn't lose Peter because he gave up. We lost him because traditional psychiatry couldn't help him get better — and the antidepressants he was prescribed only made him worse. Meru Health is the company me and my co-founders founded to build what could have helped him. Peter wasn't an outlier. Tens of millions of people share his story. They try a medication. It doesn't work, or it makes things worse. They try another. Their bloodwork is never run. Their sleep is never asked about. They learn to stop hoping. Today we launch Meru Health Advanced — a precision, whole-person psychiatry and therapy program for the patients the system has failed. A comprehensive diagnosis before a prescription. Comprehensive labs before a pill. One integrated team across psychiatry, therapy, nutrition, and lifestyle medicine. Peter didn't get that. Tens of millions of people still don't. Meru Health Advanced is our commitment to make sure the next Peter does. If you, or someone you love, has tried the standard path and not gotten better — another model exists. It opens to the public today. Find out more - link in comments ⸻ If you or someone you love is in crisis, please call or text 988 (U.S. and Canada) or contact your local emergency services.

              Exciting news: Meru Health now includes medically supervised ketogenic dietary therapy as an option within its new ADVANCED mental health care program.               • Jeffrey Pfeffer Jeffrey Pfeffer is an Influencer
                1mo
                • Report this post

                Many of the founders I have met in the mental health space, e.g. Sonia Garcia of Amae Health, have a personal story that caused them to try and fix a very ,very broken behavioral health system. 61% of U.S. counties have no practicing psychiatrists in the county. Although there was a bill passed long ago mandating equity for behavioral and physical health, we are far from there. Behavioral health predicts physical health outcomes, both through behavior and through the central nervous system's regulation of hormones and blood chemistry. Meru Health is very firmly situated in the evidence-based practice space and with their emphasis on nutrition and sleep, in the total health space. Their new product is amazing. Congratulations to Kristian Ranta and his team for this important step forward in the area of behavioral health. #mentalhealth #Meru #behavioralhealth #mentalhealthservices

                  • Brett Shrewsbury
                    1mo
                    • Report this post

                    Reposting with deep respect for our CEO Kristian and his family. Peter's story is the reason Meru Health exists — and it's the reason this launch matters. In my work with health plans across the country, I see Peter's experience repeated constantly. Members cycle through medications. Labs are never run. No one asks about sleep, nutrition, or what's actually happening underneath. They quietly stop hoping. The system has been treating symptoms while the root causes go unaddressed. Meru Health Advanced changes that — a real diagnosis before a prescription, comprehensive labs, and one integrated team across psychiatry, therapy, nutrition, and lifestyle medicine. This is what innovation backed by science actually looks like, and I'm proud to be part of bringing it to people who've been failed by the standard path.

                      • Jennifer Love
                        3w
                        • Report this post

                        One of the hardest things to watch in mental health care is patients spending years on the medication carousel without meaningful relief. Dose increases. Medication changes. Augmentation after augmentation. Side effects piling up while depression continues to impact daily life. For some patients, traditional approaches absolutely help. But for others living with treatment-resistant depression, there comes a point where we have to start asking different questions and considering different tools. That’s where interventional psychiatry can change the conversation. Treatments like: Spravato IV ketamine and TMS We are giving many patients another path forward when conventional treatment alone hasn’t been enough. What’s especially important is helping patients understand that needing a higher level of care is not a failure. Treatment-resistant depression is real, and patients deserve access to evidence-based options that go beyond the standard cycle of medication changes. Grateful to work at #HightopHealth where we continue to expand hope and possibilities for patients who have spent far too long feeling stuck. #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #MentalHealth #TRD #InterventionalPsychiatry #Spravato #KetamineTherapy #TMS #BehavioralHealth
